index.title: Case Studies
index.subtitle: Real projects with measurable business impact and technical depth

cta.contactQuestion: Want similar results for your project?

sageconnect.headline: SageConnect
sageconnect.tagline: Automating ERP Integration for 200+ Enterprise Staff
sageconnect.overview: SageConnect bridges the gap between Sage 300 ERP and supplier portals, replacing error-prone manual workflows with a fully automated integration platform. Built for an enterprise with 200+ staff members across multiple departments, the system processes CFDIs, purchase orders, payments, and supplier data in real-time.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: The company relied on manual data entry between Sage 300 ERP and their supplier portal. Staff members had to copy invoice data, purchase orders, and payment information by hand — a process that was slow, error-prone, and unsustainable as the business grew.

With 200+ employees affected across procurement, accounting, and operations, even small errors cascaded into delayed payments, supplier disputes, and reconciliation nightmares. The accounting team spent hours each week fixing mismatched records.
The existing workflow couldn't scale. Every new supplier meant more manual touchpoints, more chances for human error, and more time spent on data entry instead of strategic work.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I designed and built SageConnect as a dual-runtime system: a web dashboard for real-time monitoring and manual overrides, paired with a background processor that handles automated data synchronization around the clock.

The platform connects directly to Sage 300's database and the supplier portal's APIs, mapping data fields bidirectionally and validating every transaction before it's committed. Custom transformation rules handle edge cases in CFDI formatting, currency conversions, and tax calculations.

The system includes a monitoring dashboard that gives operations teams visibility into sync status, error rates, and processing queues — enabling them to catch and resolve issues before they impact business operations.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: Within the first month of deployment, manual data entry errors dropped by 95%. The accounting team reclaimed over 20 hours per week previously spent on reconciliation and error correction.

The platform now processes all supplier transactions automatically, achieving 100% synchronization coverage. Workflows that previously took days now complete in minutes, and the system scales effortlessly as new suppliers are onboarded.

dataFlow: Background processor runs on schedule (Task Scheduler or PM2 interval)
For each tenant: queries Sage 300 MSSQL tables for new POs, payments, and vendor data
Builds and validates payloads using Joi schemas and custom transformers
Pushes purchase orders, payments, and supplier data to Portal de Proveedores REST API
Downloads CFDIs and provider data from Portal API back to local storage
Spawns external CFDI importer process to load invoices into Sage 300

codePatterns.title: Multi-Tenant Sequential Processing
codePatterns.description: Processes multiple companies sequentially in a single Node.js process. Each tenant has its own Sage 300 database, Portal API credentials, and RFC — configured via comma-separated environment variables.

codePatterns.title: Dual-Mode Runtime
codePatterns.description: A single application runs in three modes: web-only (Express dashboard on port 3030), background-only (8 sync controllers), or combined. PM2 or Windows Task Scheduler orchestrates execution cycles.

codePatterns.title: Controller Pipeline Architecture
codePatterns.description: Eight specialized controllers execute sequentially per tenant: Provider Sync, CFDI Download, Payment Check, Payment Upload, PO Creation, PO Updates, PO Closure, and PO Cancellation — each handling one sync responsibility.

whatNext.title: What's Next
paragraphs: The current system relies on an external Windows executable (ImportaFacturasFocaltec.exe) to import CFDIs into Sage 300 — a black box with no structured error feedback, no per-invoice tracking, and no correlation between processed XMLs and created batches.

The next evolution replaces this executable entirely by integrating directly with Sage 300's REST Web API. This eliminates the dependency on Windows, enables granular error handling per invoice, and makes the entire data flow observable and testable from Node.js.

This migration also addresses critical reliability gaps: payments that upload to the portal before confirming CFDI import, silent failures in control table inserts, and XML corruption from race conditions in the addenda injection pipeline.

sagesync.headline: SageSync
sagesync.tagline: Real-Time Inventory Synchronization Between ERP and CMMS
sagesync.overview: SageSync keeps inventory data perfectly aligned between Sage 300 ERP and Fracttal CMMS, replacing manual reconciliation with automated, event-driven synchronization that achieves 99.9% data consistency.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: Inventory data lived in two disconnected systems: Sage 300 for financial tracking and Fracttal CMMS for maintenance operations. When stock was consumed for maintenance, the ERP didn't know. When purchases were recorded, the CMMS was out of sync.

The maintenance team made decisions based on stale data — ordering parts already in stock or discovering critical shortages too late. Manual reconciliation happened weekly, but errors accumulated faster than they could be fixed.
Without real-time visibility, the company couldn't accurately forecast inventory needs, leading to overstocking some items and stockouts of others.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I built SageSync as an event-driven middleware service that listens for changes in both systems and propagates updates within seconds. OAuth2 token management handles secure API authentication with automatic refresh cycles.

The service includes intelligent conflict resolution — when both systems modify the same record within the sync window, business rules determine which change takes precedence. A comprehensive monitoring dashboard tracks sync latency, error rates, and system health.
Built-in retry logic and dead-letter queues ensure no data is lost even during temporary system outages, maintaining 99.5% uptime availability.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: Sync latency dropped from days to under 30 seconds. The maintenance team now works with real-time data, eliminating surprise stockouts and reducing emergency purchases.
Manual reconciliation effort decreased by 90%, freeing the operations team to focus on strategic inventory optimization instead of data entry.

dataFlow: Sage 300 inventory changes trigger sync events
SageSync authenticates via OAuth2 with Fracttal API
Data transformer normalizes field formats between systems
Conflict resolver applies business rules for simultaneous edits
Updates are pushed to the target system with retry logic
Monitoring dashboard tracks sync health and latency metrics

codePatterns.title: OAuth2 Token Lifecycle
codePatterns.description: Automated token refresh with proactive renewal before expiration. Tokens are cached and shared across concurrent requests to minimize authentication overhead.

codePatterns.title: Event-Driven Sync Engine
codePatterns.description: Changes in either system emit events that are processed through a unified pipeline, ensuring bidirectional sync without polling overhead.

codePatterns.title: Dead Letter Queue
codePatterns.description: Failed sync operations are captured with full context for retry. After configurable retry attempts, items are escalated to the monitoring dashboard for manual review.

whatNext.title: What's Next
paragraphs: SageSync's next evolution targets a webhook-driven architecture — replacing the current polling-based synchronization with real-time event notifications from both Sage 300 and the e-commerce platform. This shift eliminates the polling interval delay and enables true sub-second sync latency for critical inventory and order data.

The observability roadmap includes containerizing the service with Docker Compose for consistent deployment, and adding a dedicated alert notification channel through email and Slack integration. When dead-letter queue items escalate beyond retry thresholds, operations teams will receive immediate alerts rather than discovering stale sync failures during manual reviews.

These improvements transform SageSync from a reliable batch-oriented bridge into a real-time integration backbone — positioning it to handle higher transaction volumes as the business scales its e-commerce operations.

cardeal.headline: Qardeal
cardeal.tagline: Modern Car Rental Platform with Integrated Payments
cardeal.overview: Qardeal is a full-featured car rental platform built with Next.js, featuring online booking, payment processing, XML invoice generation, and comprehensive admin dashboards — delivered in just 4 months.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: A car rental business was losing market share to digital-first competitors. Their booking process was phone-based, payments required in-person processing, and invoice generation was entirely manual.
Customers expected online booking with instant confirmation, digital payments, and automated invoicing. Without these capabilities, the business couldn't compete in the modern marketplace.

The admin team spent excessive time on manual operations — from tracking vehicle availability to generating compliance-required XML invoices — limiting their ability to scale the fleet or expand to new locations.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I built Qardeal as a serverless Next.js application with a focus on conversion-optimized booking flows. The platform guides users from vehicle selection through payment in a streamlined, mobile-first experience.

Payment gateway integration handles multiple payment methods with automatic retry and fraud detection. The XML invoicing module generates tax-compliant documents automatically upon booking confirmation.
An admin dashboard provides real-time fleet management, booking analytics, and revenue reporting, giving the team full operational visibility from any device.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: The platform launched in 4 months, immediately capturing online bookings. Conversion rates improved by 45% compared to the phone-based process, with 99.2% payment success rate.
Admin operations became 70% faster with automated workflows, and the team gained data-driven insights to optimize pricing and fleet allocation.

dataFlow: User browses available vehicles via SSR-rendered catalog
Booking form captures dates, insurance options, and user data
Payment gateway processes transaction with fraud checks
Booking confirmation triggers XML invoice generation
Admin dashboard updates fleet availability in real-time
Analytics pipeline aggregates revenue and conversion data

codePatterns.title: SSR-Optimized Catalog
codePatterns.description: Vehicle listings are server-side rendered for SEO, with client-side filtering for instant search. Incremental Static Regeneration keeps availability data fresh without sacrificing performance.

codePatterns.title: Payment Pipeline
codePatterns.description: Multi-step payment flow with automatic retry, webhook confirmation, and rollback on failure. Supports multiple payment methods with a unified processing interface.

codePatterns.title: XML Invoice Engine
codePatterns.description: Automated generation of tax-compliant XML invoices following Mexican CFDI standards, triggered on booking confirmation with digital signature integration.

odoo.headline: Inova ERP
odoo.tagline: Custom Odoo Modules for Automated Billing and Compliance
odoo.overview: Custom Odoo modules that automate billing processes, RFC validation, and payment controls for Inova — replacing manual processes that caused billing inaccuracies and extensive manual intervention.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: Inova's billing process was riddled with manual steps. RFC validation was done by hand, payment restrictions required manual tracking, and billing accuracy suffered from human error at every stage.
The accounting team spent hours daily verifying tax identification numbers, applying payment controls, and correcting billing errors that slipped through the manual process.
As the company grew, these manual bottlenecks became unsustainable — each new client multiplied the workload without adding proportional value.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I developed three custom Odoo modules that integrate seamlessly with the existing ERP: automated billing with configurable rules, RFC validation against the Mexican tax authority's database, and intelligent payment restriction controls.

The billing module applies complex pricing rules automatically, generates invoices on schedule, and validates all tax fields before submission. The RFC validator cross-references customer data in real-time.
Payment controls automatically flag and restrict transactions based on configurable business rules, preventing unauthorized payments while maintaining smooth operations for valid transactions.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: Billing errors dropped by 98%, and RFC validation is now 100% automated — eliminating hours of daily manual verification work.
The billing process runs 60% faster, with 85% of previously manual tasks now fully automated. The accounting team focuses on exceptions and strategy rather than routine data entry.

dataFlow: Odoo core triggers billing event on schedule or manual action
Billing module applies pricing rules and tax calculations
RFC validator checks tax ID against authority database
Payment control module evaluates restriction rules
Approved transactions generate compliant invoices
Apache POI exports financial reports to Excel format

codePatterns.title: Odoo Module Architecture
codePatterns.description: Each module follows Odoo's MVC pattern with models, views, and controllers. Business logic is encapsulated in model methods, keeping views purely presentational.

codePatterns.title: RFC Validation Pipeline
codePatterns.description: Real-time validation against the SAT database with caching for recently verified RFCs. Bulk validation mode for initial data imports processes thousands of records efficiently.

codePatterns.title: Rule-Based Payment Controls
codePatterns.description: Configurable business rules engine that evaluates payment restrictions based on client status, amount thresholds, and approval workflows.

gymmanager.headline: Gym Manager
gymmanager.tagline: Affordable Operations Platform for Local Gyms
gymmanager.overview: A cost-effective gym management platform built as a solo project, featuring membership management, KPI dashboards, and analytics reporting — deployable in just 2 hours with 90% cost savings versus commercial alternatives.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: Local gyms struggled with expensive management software that was designed for large chains. Monthly subscription costs for commercial solutions ate into already tight margins.
Gym owners tracked memberships in spreadsheets, had no visibility into KPIs like retention and churn, and couldn't generate the reports they needed for business decisions.
The available affordable tools were either too basic (just a member list) or too complex (enterprise features they'd never use), leaving a gap for a right-sized solution.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I designed Gym Manager as a lean, deployable platform that gives small gyms enterprise-level features at a fraction of the cost. The Next.js frontend provides a responsive admin interface that works on any device.

The KPI dashboard calculates and visualizes key metrics: member retention, churn rate, revenue trends, and attendance patterns. Automated alerts notify owners when metrics cross configurable thresholds.
Built on PostgreSQL for reliable data storage and deployed on Vercel for minimal hosting costs, the entire platform can be set up for a new gym in under 2 hours.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: Gym owners save 90% compared to commercial alternatives while gaining better insights into their business. The platform deployed in 2 hours versus weeks for traditional solutions.
Admin efficiency improved by 75%, and gyms using the KPI dashboard saw 20% improvement in member retention through data-driven engagement strategies.

dataFlow: Admin registers members through responsive web interface
PostgreSQL stores membership, payment, and attendance data
KPI engine calculates retention, churn, and revenue metrics
Dashboard renders real-time visualizations of business health
Alert system monitors thresholds and notifies via dashboard
Report generator exports data for accounting and tax purposes

codePatterns.title: KPI Calculation Engine
codePatterns.description: Server-side calculation of business metrics using PostgreSQL aggregation queries. Metrics are cached and refreshed incrementally to keep dashboards responsive.

codePatterns.title: Rapid Deployment Pipeline
codePatterns.description: Environment-specific configuration with sensible defaults allows a new gym to be set up by changing a single config file and deploying to Vercel.

codePatterns.title: Responsive Admin Interface
codePatterns.description: Mobile-first design ensures gym owners can manage operations from their phone on the gym floor, with touch-optimized controls and progressive data loading.

carrytrade.headline: Carry Trade Algo
carrytrade.tagline: Algorithmic Forex Strategy — Live 94-Day Validation on $100K
carrytrade.overview: A systematic algorithmic trading system that exploits interest rate differentials across 6 JPY currency pairs. Built from scratch in Python with 473 tests, the system runs 24/5 on AWS with hourly decision cycles, adaptive risk management, ML signal filtering, and real-time Telegram monitoring. Currently in day 41 of a 94-day live validation protocol on OANDA paper trading — no real money at risk.

problem.title: The Hypothesis
paragraphs: Carry trades exploit interest rate gaps between currencies — borrow in low-rate JPY (~0%) and hold higher-yield currencies (USD, AUD, GBP at 4-5%). The spread generates daily swap income even if price doesn't move. Historically, this is one of the most persistent edges in forex.

But manual carry trading is emotional and inconsistent. Human traders hold too long in downtrends, exit too early in uptrends, and can't monitor 6 pairs around the clock. The 2024 JPY carry unwind wiped out months of gains for discretionary traders in a single week.

The hypothesis: a systematic, trend-following approach with strict multi-layer risk management can capture carry income while avoiding the catastrophic drawdowns that historically devastate carry traders — preserving capital by only entering when trend, momentum, and regime all align.

solution.title: The System
paragraphs: The V3 strategy enters only when price trades above the 50-day MA which itself is above the 200-day MA (golden cross confirms uptrend), the pair pays positive swap, RSI is below overbought, and the market regime is favorable. This multi-gate approach means the system stays flat during downtrends — exactly when carry traders get crushed.

Risk management operates through 5 independent layers: ATR-based position sizing adjusts for volatility, Kelly criterion optimizes bet size, regime detection scales exposure, correlation monitoring prevents over-concentration in correlated JPY pairs, and quadratic drawdown decay progressively reduces risk as losses mount. Three circuit breakers (daily -3%, weekly -7%, max DD -20%) provide hard stops.

The operational stack runs hourly via APScheduler on Docker/AWS: each tick fetches 300 candles from OANDA, runs the strategy pipeline through an ML bandit gate (Thompson Sampling), executes via limit orders, persists all state to SQLite (survives container restarts), and reports to Telegram for real-time monitoring.

impact.title: Live Results
paragraphs: Day 41 of 94 on OANDA paper trading: $100,086.29 equity from $100,000 start. Peak equity reached $100,124.56. The system achieved a 70% win rate across 30 clean trades with a Sharpe ratio of 3.21 on active trading days.

The system correctly identified and avoided the February JPY strengthening, holding zero positions during the downtrend — exactly the capital preservation behavior that distinguishes systematic from discretionary trading. Monte Carlo simulation across 5,000 runs shows 0% probability of ruin and a 5.9% median annualized return.

dataFlow: APScheduler triggers hourly tick during forex market hours (Sun 22:00 - Fri 22:00 UTC)
Reconciler syncs internal state with OANDA broker positions (broker = source of truth)
Data fetcher pulls 300 H1 candles per pair from OANDA v20 REST API
V3 Strategy evaluates golden cross, swap rates, RSI, and regime for entry/exit signals
ML Bandit gate (Thompson Sampling) decides TAKE or SKIP based on 14 contextual features
Dynamic sizer calculates position units: Kelly × ATR × regime × correlation × drawdown decay
Exit manager checks 7 conditions per tick: stop-loss, trailing, time-based, profit-scaled, regime change, trend reversal
Orders placed as limits at bid price (saves 1-2 pips vs market orders)
Circuit breakers evaluate daily/weekly/max-DD thresholds — halt trading if breached
SQLite WAL-mode persistence saves all state (survives container restarts)
Telegram bot dispatches alerts and responds to /status, /trends, /positions, /health commands

codePatterns.title: Multi-Layer Risk Pipeline
codePatterns.description: Position sizing flows through 5 independent risk factors multiplied together: Kelly criterion (optimal bet size from win rate), ATR volatility scaling (less size when volatile), regime multiplier (0-100% based on trend + volatility state), correlation factor (reduces size when JPY pairs are >0.7 correlated), and quadratic drawdown decay (15% DD = 50% size). Three circuit breakers provide hard stops.

codePatterns.title: Regime-Adaptive Strategy
codePatterns.description: A composite regime detector classifies markets into 4 states using ADX (trend strength) and ATR (volatility): TREND_LOW_VOL (full size), TREND_HIGH_VOL (75%), RANGE_LOW_VOL (25%), RANGE_HIGH_VOL (0%). Weekly Bayesian optimization via Optuna retrains parameters per regime on recent data.

codePatterns.title: Operational Resilience
codePatterns.description: SQLite WAL-mode state store survives Docker container restarts. Hourly reconciliation with OANDA detects position mismatches. Economic calendar integration blackouts entries ±1h around high-impact events (FOMC, BOJ). Watchdog monitors heartbeats and escalates via Telegram on missed ticks.

gallery.title: Performance Dashboard
gallery.subtitle: Live charts from the 94-day validation protocol and historical backtesting

charts.label: Equity Curve — Forward Test
charts.description: Live equity with drawdown overlay. Red zone marks a bug period (excluded from results).

charts.label: V3 Backtest — 2023-2024
charts.description: Portfolio equity across 6 JPY pairs on historical data. +5.18% return over 2 years.

charts.label: P&L by Currency Pair
charts.description: AUD/JPY leads performance. GBP/JPY is the only negative pair — higher volatility profile.

charts.label: Monte Carlo Simulation (n=5000)
charts.description: Return distribution across 5,000 randomized simulations. Median 5.9%, 0% ruin probability.

charts.label: Pair Correlation Matrix
charts.description: JPY pair correlations range 0.40-0.93. The system reduces position size when correlation exceeds 0.7.

charts.label: Stress Test Scenarios
charts.description: Portfolio behavior under 5 historical crisis scenarios including flash crashes and rate reversals.

charts.label: Cumulative P&L (Trade Sequence)
charts.description: Running total profit across sequential trades during the live validation period.

charts.label: Exit Reason Analysis
charts.description: Distribution of exit triggers: trailing stops, trend reversals, and time-based exits.

whatNext.title: What's Next
paragraphs: 53 days remain in the 94-day validation protocol. The system needs 50+ completed trades for statistical significance and must maintain <15% drawdown to pass. Current trajectory: 30 clean trades with 70% win rate and 0.53% max drawdown.

After protocol completion, the Real Money Readiness checklist requires: regime detection as a hard entry gate (not just sizing), ML bandit activation (currently in shadow mode learning from outcomes), VIX > 25 hard block, BOJ meeting 48-hour blackout, and weekend gap protection.

Capital deployment follows a phased approach: $5-10K initial allocation with strict position limits, scaling to $20-25K after 3 months of live performance, with full deployment only after 6+ months of consistent, auditable results.

cleany.headline: Cleany
cleany.tagline: Multi-Tenant SaaS Platform for House Cleaning Businesses
cleany.overview: Cleany is a comprehensive SaaS platform that helps cleaning service owners manage customers, workers, jobs, scheduling, invoicing, and payroll in one unified system. Built as a monorepo with three independent apps — a Fastify REST API, a React SPA with offline-first worker mobile, and a Next.js marketing site — the platform targets Latin American markets with full bilingual support.

problem.title: The Problem
paragraphs: House cleaning businesses in Latin America managed operations through spreadsheets, WhatsApp groups, and paper schedules. Workers didn't know their jobs until a phone call, customers had no visibility into upcoming visits, and owners couldn't track revenue or worker performance.

Existing solutions were either too expensive for small operators, English-only, or designed for enterprise facilities management — not the lean, mobile-first workflow that cleaning businesses actually need.
Field workers often had unreliable connectivity at customer locations, making cloud-only solutions impractical. Any missed job update meant lost revenue and scheduling chaos.

solution.title: The Solution
paragraphs: I designed Cleany as a multi-tenant platform where each cleaning business gets their own isolated workspace. The admin dashboard handles the full lifecycle: customer management, worker scheduling on a weekly grid, recurring job generation, invoicing with Stripe payment links, and payroll calculation.

Workers onboard in 60 seconds via QR code scan and PIN — no app store needed. The offline-first progressive web app uses Service Workers and IndexedDB to cache job data, with a sync queue that retries writes automatically when connectivity returns.
The marketing landing page at cleany.pro is powered by Next.js and Sanity CMS for dynamic blog content, with Stripe checkout integration for SaaS subscription management.

impact.title: The Impact
paragraphs: The platform consolidates what previously required 4-5 disconnected tools into a single system. Worker onboarding dropped from hours of training to 60 seconds with QR scan, and the offline-first approach ensures zero missed job updates in the field.

Built-in payroll eliminates manual calculations, Stripe integration automates payment collection, and the recurring schedule engine generates jobs automatically — letting owners focus on growing their business instead of managing spreadsheets.

dataFlow: Admin manages customers, workers, and services through the React SPA dashboard
React SPA communicates with Fastify REST API via Axios with JWT authentication
Fastify routes validate requests with JSON schemas and delegate to service layer
Prisma ORM queries PostgreSQL with tenant isolation on every operation
Recurring schedule engine auto-generates work orders based on configurable patterns
Workers receive jobs via offline-first PWA with IndexedDB cache and sync queue
Stripe webhooks process SaaS subscriptions and customer payment confirmations
Resend and Twilio fire-and-forget notifications for invoices and job alerts

codePatterns.title: Multi-Tenant Isolation
codePatterns.description: Every API query filters by tenant_id extracted from the JWT. Prisma middleware enforces row-level isolation, and response serializers strip sensitive fields — ensuring zero data leakage between business accounts.

codePatterns.title: Offline-First Sync Engine
codePatterns.description: Service Workers cache job data with 24-hour TTL and 50-item cap. An IndexedDB write queue captures mutations offline, retrying with exponential backoff (3 attempts) when connectivity returns via the online event.

codePatterns.title: QR + PIN Worker Authentication
codePatterns.description: Workers scan a unique QR token that identifies them, then set a PIN on first login. Refresh token rotation with reuse detection prevents session hijacking, and account lockout triggers after 5 failed attempts.

gallery.title: Live Preview
gallery.subtitle: Screenshots from the landing page, admin dashboard, and demo environment

charts.label: Landing Page — cleany.pro
charts.description: Marketing site built with Next.js and Sanity CMS. Features pricing, blog, and Stripe checkout.

charts.label: Admin Dashboard
charts.description: Business owner's overview with key metrics, recent activity, and quick actions.

charts.label: Weekly Schedule
charts.description: Visual job scheduling grid with group-based worker assignment and recurring job generation.

charts.label: Job Management
charts.description: Work order tracking with status pipeline: Pending, In Progress, Completed. ATR-based pricing snapshots.

charts.label: Customer Management
charts.description: Full customer profiles with addresses, service frequency, billing preferences, and notification settings.

charts.label: Sign Up Flow
charts.description: Onboarding experience for new cleaning business owners. Multi-tenant workspace creation.

whatNext.title: What's Next
paragraphs: The next milestone focuses on proof-of-work features: photo evidence capture (before/after photos per job), GPS clock-in verification to confirm workers are on-site, and push notifications so workers receive real-time job alerts without polling the app.
A customer-facing portal will let end customers view their cleaning schedule, accept quotes, request reschedules, and pay invoices — closing the loop between cleaning businesses and their clients.
The platform will also add a lead capture funnel on the landing page with analytics tracking, converting the marketing site from informational to revenue-generating.
